summary: Delete check.rs's dead pre-engine lint scaffold ‚Äî `trait Rule`/`run`/`OneErrorPerSkill` and the unused diagnostic-span channel; fix the false module header

per.section: Decision: kill the heuristic rule registry

edit.description: Delete the dead pre-engine lint architecture `engine.rs` superseded: `trait Rule` (~227), `fn run` (~237), the test-only `OneErrorPerSkill` (~335) and its two `run` tests; and the never-used diagnostic-span channel ‚Äî `Diagnostic::span` (~154), `with_span` (~194, zero callers), the `labels()` override (~216, always None), and the now-unused `SourceSpan`/`LabeledSpan` imports (~26). Rewrite the false module header (~11-17) that still sells `Rule::check`/`run` as the architecture. KEEP `Workspace`/`Diagnostic` struct/`Severity`/`render`/`any_error` ‚Äî zero production edits.

acceptance: check.rs carries only the live surface (Workspace/Diagnostic/Severity/render/any_error); the dead Rule registry and the always-None span channel are gone; the module header describes the engine, not the retired registry; no production behavior change; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Inbox CHECK-CLEANUP (D1+D3). engine.rs replaced the heuristic registry (specs/10-contracts.md); the trait/run/OneErrorPerSkill scaffold and the diagnostic-span plumbing are pure dead code. check.rs-only ‚Äî fully disjoint from the rest of the wave.

summary: Reject `[kind.*.entities]` uniformly in both compose kind-parse paths (custom-kind silently drops it today) and fix the false `folded in elsewhere` comment

per.section: Decision: unknown keys are rejected, not ignored

edit.description: Spec 2bc1013 dropped the phantom `[kind.<name>.entities]` table. Make `parse_custom_kind` (~1075) reject unknown keys the way `parse_kind_layer` (~1031) already does ‚Äî iterate the table, allow only `governs`/`extraction`/`clause`/`relationships`, and reject any stray key (a leftover `[kind.*.entities]`, a typo) as a load error rather than silently dropping it. Fix the false comment (~68, `[kind.<name>.entities] ... folded in elsewhere`): nodes derive from `features.id`; there is no separate entities table.

tests.asserts: a custom-kind `[kind.<name>]` declaration carrying a stray subtable/key (e.g. `entities`) is a load error, exactly as `parse_kind_layer` already rejects it.

acceptance: Both compose kind-parse paths reject an unrecognized key on a `[kind.<name>]` table uniformly; a leftover `[kind.*.entities]` fails loudly instead of being silently dropped; the stale `folded in elsewhere` comment is corrected; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Inbox KIND-ENTITIES-RECONCILE (R1). Today `parse_kind_layer` rejects unknown keys but `parse_custom_kind` pulls only its known parts and drops the rest ‚Äî align them (anti-silent-gap, specs/10-contracts.md unknown-key decision). Consolidation shipped (19333aa); compose.rs-only. MATCHSELECTOR-ROLE-DROP serializes after this (shared compose.rs).

summary: Remove the write-only `RollupEntry.body_hash` ‚Äî no production reader; law-5 provenance is `{source_path, import_hash}` only

edit.description: Drop the `RollupEntry.body_hash` field (~129), its three `sha256_hex` computations (~306/345/460), and its emit into the lock row (~573); rewrite the `five-column row` doc (~541) to the four columns production actually uses (`source_path`/`import_hash`/`last_applied`).
Drop the two `re_add` rewrites of `body_hash` (~1037/1055) and the doc mentions (~66/997) ‚Äî nothing reads it back.
Regenerate the `coordinate_import_surface` insta snapshot (tests/snapshots/acceptance__coordinate_import_surface.snap) ‚Äî the emitted lock row loses its `body_hash` column.

tests.asserts: the generated lock (lock.toml) row carries four columns ‚Äî source_path/import_hash/last_applied ‚Äî with body_hash gone; the snapshot is regenerated.

acceptance: `RollupEntry` carries no `body_hash`; the lock emits the four columns production reads; `re_add` no longer rewrites it; the `coordinate_import_surface` snapshot is updated; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Inbox BODY-HASH-DROP (D2). Write-only field, no production reader; law-5 provenance is {source_path, import_hash} (specs/20-surface.md). Touches import.rs + drift.rs + the acceptance snapshot. SHA256-HOIST serializes after (shared import.rs/drift.rs).

summary: Remove the never-read `Features.companions` projection (its sole consumer companion-refs was killed as unsound); keep `Skill::companions` for the byte-faithful copy

per.section: Decision: kill the heuristic rule registry

edit.description: Remove the `Features.companions` field (~175), its population in `skill_features` (~243-247), the `rule_features` placeholder (~279), and the two `assert!(features.companions.is_empty())` test asserts (~640/696).
Remove the `companions: Vec::new()` test-helper placeholder (~380) and the stale `companions are unused by these clauses` comment (~363).
Remove the `companions: Vec::new()` placeholder in the `features()` test helper (~749).
Remove the `companions: Vec::new()` test-helper placeholder (~160).
Remove the `companions: Vec::new()` test-helper placeholder (~583).
Remove the `companions: Vec::new()` placeholder in the composed-Unit `Features` (~451).

acceptance: `Features` has no `companions` field; every constructor across extract/engine/roster/coverage/graph/kind is updated; `Skill::companions` is retained (import.rs byte-faithful copy); cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Inbox FEATURES-COMPANIONS-DROP (D4). companion-refs was killed as unsound ‚Üí a companion-reference clause is inadmissible (specs/10-contracts.md), so the feature projection is dead. Field + ~6 Vec::new() placeholders. KEEP Skill::companions (byte-faithful copy). Shares engine.rs with DEPENDENCY-EXISTS-FENCE and roster.rs with MATCHSELECTOR-ROLE-DROP ‚Äî both serialize after.

summary: Reject `Predicate::DependencyExists` in admissibility so a hand-authored clause fails loud, not a silent `Indeterminate` no-op the spec fenced

edit.description: Treat `Predicate::DependencyExists` (~203) as a held-back predicate (like `pattern`): recognizable when parsed, but flagged inadmissible rather than accepted as a working clause.

In `admissibility`, emit an inadmissibility finding for a `DependencyExists` clause (a held predicate with no decidable reference syntax/extractor), so a hand-authored `dependency-exists` fails loudly. Today `decide` returns `Outcome::Indeterminate` for it (~333) ‚Äî a silent no-op law 1 forbids. Update the inline test asserting `run(DependencyExists, ..)` is empty (~787) to the new fenced behavior.

tests.asserts: a contract clause using `dependency-exists` fails admissibility (a held predicate), never silently decides `Indeterminate`.

acceptance: A hand-authored `dependency-exists` clause is an admissibility violation (fenced like `pattern`), never a silent `Indeterminate` no-op; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Inbox DEPENDENCY-EXISTS-FENCE (R2). Spec 2bc1013 fenced dependency-exists as held-back (no decidable reference syntax/extractor yet); engine.rs:333 returns Outcome::Indeterminate = the silent no-op law 1 forbids. Mirror the `pattern` fence. blockedBy FEATURES-COMPANIONS-DROP (shared engine.rs).

summary: Hoist the duplicate `sha256_hex` defs (skill/rule/import/drift) into one shared `&[u8]` helper ‚Äî only the sha256 hoist, not the frontmatter round-trip helpers

new.description: A small shared `sha256_hex(bytes: &[u8]) -> String` util ‚Äî the single home for the SHA-256 hex the import_hash/drift anchors compute (specs/20-surface.md law 5). Generic `&[u8]`, no kind-typing lost.

edit.description: Add `pub(crate) mod hash;` beside the other modules ‚Äî this entry owns the `mod` line.
Delete the local `sha256_hex` (~350); call `crate::hash::sha256_hex`.
Delete the local `sha256_hex` (~358); call `crate::hash::sha256_hex`.
Delete the local `sha256_hex` (~597); call `crate::hash::sha256_hex`.
Delete the local `sha256_hex` (~322); call `crate::hash::sha256_hex`.

acceptance: One shared `sha256_hex`; skill/rule/import/drift all call it; no duplicate defs remain; hash output is byte-identical (no snapshot churn); cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Inbox SHA256-HOIST (U2-sha256). Do ONLY the sha256 hoist. The frontmatter round-trip helpers (split_frontmatter, pod_scalar_to_string, ...) stay duplicated across skill.rs/rule.rs per the documented 'one artifact kind per module' tradeoff ‚Äî accepted debt (.claude/rules/rust.md), not this entry. blockedBy BODY-HASH-DROP (shared import.rs/drift.rs).

summary: Drop the `role:` marker opt-in ‚Äî the `MatchSelector::Role` variant + parse arms + roster read + empty-marker admissibility guard; `satisfies` is the sole artifact-side fill

edit.description: Drop the `MatchSelector::Role` variant (~461), its `selector_from` parse arm (~1497), the two test constructions (~1982/2427), and the doc reference (~188).

Drop the `matches()` `Role` arm (~463-464, reading `features.field("role")`), the empty-marker admissibility guard (~304-315, pure seam-policing), and the `MatchSelector::Role` doc mentions (~44/266/445). Adjust the `features()` test helper and the role-marker tests (~735/827) to the name/glob path.
Remove/adjust the role-marker roster tests ‚Äî fill is now `satisfies` opt-in or a `match` name/glob selector only.

tests.asserts: a requirement is filled only via a `satisfies` opt-in or a `match` name/glob selector; the removed `role:` marker path is gone and its empty-marker guard with it.

acceptance: No `MatchSelector::Role`; the role-marker admissibility guard is removed; a requirement's fill is `satisfies` (artifact-side) or `match` name/glob (contract-side) only, per the consolidated fill vocabulary; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Inbox MATCHSELECTOR-ROLE-DROP (U1), the accretion residue. Post-consolidation, satisfies is the sole artifact-side opt-in; match=name/glob is contract-side (specs/10-contracts.md). MatchSelector::Role reads Features.field("role"), no struct field ‚Äî clean delete over the unified Requirement. blockedBy KIND-ENTITIES-RECONCILE (compose.rs); also shares roster.rs with FEATURES-COMPANIONS-DROP, so held out of that wave.

summary: Custom-kind units join coverage ‚Äî read `[representation]` `satisfies` in `kind::Unit` instead of hardcoding it empty

gate.reason: follow-on; no story yet demands custom-kind coverage participation ‚Äî deferred on priority

edit.description: Populate `Features.satisfies` for a custom-kind `Unit` from its projected `[representation]` rather than the hardcoded `satisfies: Vec::new()` (the commented gap ~451-455), so a custom kind (temper's own `spec` corpus) can opt into requirements ‚Äî coverage is kind-blind by design. Confirm `import`'s custom-unit projection emits `[representation]` for the read to find.
A custom-kind unit declaring `satisfies` covers a requirement, exactly as a skill/rule does.

tests.asserts: a custom-kind unit's `satisfies` participates in coverage (kind-blind).

acceptance: A custom-kind unit's authored `satisfies` participates in coverage exactly as a skill/rule does; temper's own `spec` corpus can opt into requirements; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Inbox DEFER (1). kind::Unit hardcodes `satisfies=Vec::new()` (kind.rs commented gap). Coverage is kind-blind (spec), so the seam is legitimate ‚Äî deferred only on priority. CONSOLIDATE-REQUIREMENT shipped (19333aa); on revival rebase onto the unified Requirement and past FEATURES-COMPANIONS-DROP (which edits kind.rs/coverage.rs). Revive when the dogfood needs the spec corpus in coverage.

summary: Ship acquisition channels ‚Äî npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install ‚Äî auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: npm entry with platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's distribution pattern); a small launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/50-distribution.md`).
Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected.

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infrastructure, mostly outside `src/` ‚Äî the flume cargo gates (fmt/clippy/test) can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human-held creds (npm org, tap repo, signing). Parked until a human sets those up; the mechanical wrapper/launcher is buildable once they exist. `(binary-bootstrap)` is RESOLVED ‚Äî this is execution, not a fork.

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind ‚Äî `.claude/agents/*.md`, typed frontmatter + byte-faithful body, with its own decidable contract

gate.reason: reframe deprioritizes more built-in kinds; revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The Agent IR ‚Äî the typed model for a Claude Code subagent at `.claude/agents/<name>.md`, mirroring `src/rule.rs` end-to-end. Split YAML frontmatter into typed fields (`name`, `description`, optional `tools`/`model`) with every unknown key preserved verbatim in `extra`, a byte-faithful markdown body, and `provenance { source_path, import_hash }`. Ship `from_source_file`, `to_meta_document`, and `from_surface_dir`. Body copied byte-for-byte; only the header rewritten. Identity is the path (file stem), not an internal field (`specs/10-contracts.md`).

The built-in decidable floor for the `agent` kind, embedded like `contracts/rule.toml`. Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]` (harness fact); `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores (`globs`/`alwaysApply`); an advisory `max_lines`. No heuristic clause (`specs/00-intent.md` law 3).
Fixture-driven proof mirroring `tests/rule_contract.rs`: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an insta import round-trip proving idempotence.

edit.description: Add `pub mod agent;` beside the other artifact-kind modules (this entry owns the `pub mod` line).
Extend `Workspace` with `agents: Vec<Agent>` loaded under `<dir>/agents/*`, and add an `Agent(#[from] AgentError)` arm to `WorkspaceError`. Update the load tests.
Add `pub fn agent_features(&Agent) -> Features` mirroring `rule_features`: typed fields as scalars, every `extra` key folded into the name-keyed map, `body_lines` over the byte-faithful body.

Discover `.claude/agents/*.md`, project each to `<into>/agents/<name>/{meta.toml, AGENT.md}`, and add a `[[agent]]` lock row per agent ‚Äî parallel to the rule path. Extend the idempotence snapshot to cover an agents fixture.

Wire the `agent` kind into `gate()`: embed `BUILTIN_AGENT_CONTRACT`, extract `agent_features` over `ws.agents`, compose the effective contract (floor ‚äï layer), extend the admissibility + conformance streams, add `"agent"` to `by_kind`, and add the agent floor to `schema`'s `floors`.

tests.asserts: Each decidable clause of contracts/agent.toml fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the embedded agent contract (conformance) and the agent contract passes admissibility; `temper import` scans, projects, and round-trips agents idempotently with `[[agent]]` lock rows; `temper schema --kind agent` emits the agent contract; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Deferred by the requirements/satisfies reframe: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction; value is meaningful contracts + the representation surface. Revive only if a story demands the `agent` kind ‚Äî files/acceptance stay valid; mirrors the rule kind end-to-end. On revival re-check its shared-surface blast radius (lib/check/extract/import/main) serializes against any then-open entry.
